data.name: AllowInCustomizationsForOmittedFieldsTitle
data.value: Explicitly set AllowInCustomizations for excluded fields

data.name: AllowInCustomizationsForOmittedFieldsMessageFormat
data.value: Field '{0}' is not exposed on any page and should explicitly define AllowInCustomizations.

data.name: AllowInCustomizationsForOmittedFieldsDescription
data.value: Fields that are not included on pages can still be exposed through page customizations. To avoid unintended visibility or customization behavior, the AllowInCustomizations property must be set explicitly for such fields.

data.name: CaptionRequiredTitle
data.value: Captions must be defined on user-facing objects and controls

data.name: CaptionRequiredMessageFormat
data.value: A user-facing object or control is missing a Caption.

data.name: CaptionRequiredDescription
data.value: All user-facing application objects and controls must explicitly define a Caption to ensure a consistent, accessible, and localizable user interface.

data.name: ConfirmImplementConfirmManagementTitle
data.value: Confirm() must be implemented through the "Confirm Management" codeunit

data.name: ConfirmImplementConfirmManagementMessageFormat
data.value: Confirm() must be implemented through the "Confirm Management" codeunit from the System Application.

data.name: ConfirmImplementConfirmManagementDescription
data.value: Direct calls to Confirm() bypass the safeguards and features provided by the Confirm Management codeunit in the System Application. Using Confirm Management ensures automatic validation of IsGuiAllowed(), prevents runtime failures in non-GUI contexts, and allows explicit control over the default response (true or false).

data.name: ConfirmImplementConfirmManagementDescriptionCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Refactor to use "Confirm Management" codeunit

data.name: DuplicateToolTipBetweenPageAndTableTitle
data.value: Duplicate ToolTip between page and table field.

data.name: DuplicateToolTipBetweenPageAndTableMessageFormat
data.value: The ToolTip property of page field '{0}'' and it's table field '{1}'' have the same value and therefore the page field's ToolTip can be removed.

data.name: DuplicateToolTipBetweenPageAndTableDescription
data.value: Informs the user that it is better to only apply the ToolTip property on the table field when the page fields ToolTip is the same.

data.name: EmptyCaptionLockedTitle
data.value: Empty captions should be locked

data.name: EmptyCaptionLockedMessageFormat
data.value: The Caption property is empty but not locked. Empty captions should be marked with Locked = true to prevent translation entries.

data.name: EmptyCaptionLockedDescription
data.value: When a Caption property is intentionally left empty, it should always be marked with Locked = true. An empty caption without locking is still extracted into translation files, resulting in meaningless entries.

data.name: EmptyCaptionLockedCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Set Locked property to true

data.name: EnumEmptyValueHasCaptionTitle
data.value: Empty Enum value should not have a Caption property specified.

data.name: EnumEmptyValueHasCaptionMessageFormat
data.value: The empty enum value defines a Caption, which will never be shown or used by the client

data.name: EnumEmptyValueHasCaptionDescription
data.value: Enum values with an empty name act as sentinel or placeholder values in Business Central. These values are not selectable, not accessible from AL code, and never displayed in the client.

data.name: EnumValueHasEmptyCaptionTitle
data.value: Enum value must have non-empty Caption to be selectable in the client.

data.name: EnumValueHasEmptyCaptionMessageFormat
data.value: The enum value '{0}' has an empty Caption and will not be shown or be selectable in the client.

data.name: EnumValueHasEmptyCaptionDescription
data.value: Enum values with a non-empty name are intended to be selectable and visible in the Business Central client. If the Caption is empty, the value is hidden from the UI, leading to unexpected behavior and incomplete user interaction.

data.name: FieldGroupsRequiredTitle
data.value: DropDown and Brick fieldgroups must be defined

data.name: FieldGroupsRequiredMessageFormat
data.value: Table '{0}' is missing the required '{1}' fieldgroup.

data.name: FieldGroupsRequiredDescription
data.value: Table objects must explicitly define both the DropDown and Brick fieldgroups.

data.name: GlobalLanguageImplementTranslationHelperTitle
data.value: GlobalLanguage() must be implemented through the "Translation Helper" codeunit

data.name: GlobalLanguageImplementTranslationHelperMessageFormat
data.value: GlobalLanguage() must be implemented through the "Translation Helper" codeunit from the Base Application.

data.name: GlobalLanguageImplementTranslationHelperDescription
data.value: Direct use of GlobalLanguage() leads to verbose, error-prone code and often requires manual state management, hardcoded language IDs, or duplicated logic. The Translation Helper codeunit from the Base Application centralizes global language handling by automatically preserving and restoring the previous language, supporting both LanguageId and LanguageCode, and allowing a safe reset to the default application language without hardcoding values such as 1033.

data.name: GlobalLanguageImplementTranslationHelperCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Refactor to use "Translation Helper" codeunit

data.name: LabelLockedMustHaveTokSuffixTitle
data.value: Locked Label must have a suffix Tok.

data.name: LabelLockedMustHaveTokSuffixMessageFormat
data.value: Label '{0}' has the Locked property set to true but is not suffixed with 'Tok'.

data.name: LabelLockedMustHaveTokSuffixDescription
data.value: A label with the Locked property set to true is explicitly marked as non-translatable and represents a hardcoded value. To make this intent explicit and consistent, locked labels must be suffixed with 'Tok'.

data.name: LabelTokenRequiresTokSuffixTitle
data.value: Use the 'Tok' suffix for token labels.

data.name: LabelTokenRequiresTokSuffixMessageFormat
data.value: Label '{0}' represents a token but does not use the required 'Tok' suffix.

data.name: LabelTokenRequiresTokSuffixDescription
data.value: When a label value is identical to its name, the label represents a fixed token rather than translatable text. Such labels must use the 'Tok' suffix to make this intent explicit.

data.name: LabelWithTokSuffixMustBeLockedTitle
data.value: Labels suffixed with Tok must be locked.

data.name: LabelWithTokSuffixMustBeLockedMessageFormat
data.value: Label '{0}' is suffixed with 'Tok' but does not have the Locked property set to true.

data.name: LabelWithTokSuffixMustBeLockedDescription
data.value: Labels ending with the suffix 'Tok' represent tokens: hardcoded, non-translatable values. Such labels must explicitly have the Locked property set to true to prevent translation.

data.name: LabelWithTokSuffixMustBeLockedCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Set Locked property to true

data.name: InstallAndUpgradeCodeunitsShouldBeInternalTitle
data.value: Install and Upgrade codeunits should have Access set to Internal

data.name: InstallAndUpgradeCodeunitsShouldBeInternalMessageFormat
data.value: Set Access property to Internal for Install and Upgrade codeunits.

data.name: InstallAndUpgradeCodeunitsShouldBeInternalDescription
data.value: Install and Upgrade codeunits are intended for lifecycle execution only and are not designed to expose reusable APIs. Declaring them as Public unnecessarily broadens their surface area and increases coupling between extensions.

data.name: InstallAndUpgradeCodeunitsShouldBeInternalCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Set Access property to Internal

data.name: IntegrationEventInInternalCodeunitTitle
data.value: Integration events must not be declared in codeunits with Access set to Internal

data.name: IntegrationEventInInternalCodeunitMessageFormat
data.value: Integration event '{0}' is declared in a codeunit with Access = Interna`, making it inaccessible to extensions. Remove the Access property or declare the event as an InternalEvent.

data.name: IntegrationEventInInternalCodeunitDescription
data.value: Integration events are designed to be consumed by extensions and therefore must be accessible outside the defining app. When an IntegrationEvent is declared in a codeunit with Access = Internal, the event cannot be subscribed to by extensions, rendering it ineffective.

data.name: IntegrationEventInInternalCodeunitRemoveAccessPropertyCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove property Access = Internal

data.name: IntegrationEventInInternalCodeunitChangeToInternalEventCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Refactor from IntegrationEvent to InternalEvent

data.name: LineSeparatorShouldUseTypeHelperTitle
data.value: Use the (CR)LFSeparator from the "Type Helper" codeunit.

data.name: LineSeparatorShouldUseTypeHelperMessageFormat
data.value: Use the (CR)LFSeparator from the "Type Helper" codeunit from the Base Application to define a line feed (LF) or carriage return (CR) variable.

data.name: LineSeparatorShouldUseTypeHelperDescription
data.value: Avoid manually creating helper methods or assigning character values (e.g., Char := 10 or Text[1] := 10) to define line feed (LF) or carriage return (CR) variables. Instead, use the LFSeparator and CRLFSeparator constants provided by the "Type Helper" codeunit from the Base Application.

data.name: LookupPageIdAndDrillDownPageIdTitle
data.value: DrillDownPageId and LookupPageId property must be defined for tables used in list pages

data.name: LookupPageIdAndDrillDownPageIdMessageFormat
data.value: Table '{0}' is used in a list page but does not define both the DrillDownPageId and LookupPageId properties.

data.name: LookupPageIdAndDrillDownPageIdDescription
data.value: When a table is used as the source for a list page, users expect to be able to drill down into records and open lookups in a consistent and predictable way. The DrillDownPageId and LookupPageId table properties define which pages are used for these interactions.

data.name: NotBlankRequiredOnPrimaryKeyFieldTitle
data.value: Single-field primary key requires the NotBlank property

data.name: NotBlankRequiredOnPrimaryKeyFieldMessageFormat
data.value: NotBlank property should be set explicitly for tables with a single-field primary key.

data.name: NotBlankRequiredOnPrimaryKeyFieldDescription
data.value: In tables that define a primary key consisting of a single field of type Code or Text, the primary key field must explicitly set the NotBlank property.

data.name: NotBlankRequiredOnPrimaryKeyFieldCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Set NotBlank property to true

data.name: NotBlankNotAllowedOnPrimaryKeyFieldTitle
data.value: Set NotBlank property to false when 'No. Series' TableRelation exists

data.name: NotBlankNotAllowedOnPrimaryKeyFieldMessageFormat
data.value: The NotBlank property should be set to false (or removed) if a TableRelation of 'No. Series' is present on the table.

data.name: NotBlankNotAllowedOnPrimaryKeyFieldDescription
data.value: When the 'No. Series' TableRelation is present on the table, assigning the primary key might occur after inserting the record, which could cause an error due to the NotBlank property.

data.name: NotBlankNotAllowedOnPrimaryKeyFieldCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Set NotBlank property to false

data.name: PermissionSetCaptionLengthTitle
data.value: The Caption of permissionset objects should not exceed the maximum length

data.name: PermissionSetCaptionLengthMessageFormat
data.value: The Caption of permissionset objects should not exceed {0} characters. Use MaxLength={0} or Locked=true to ensure there are no translations that exceed this limit.

data.name: PermissionSetCaptionLengthDescription
data.value: PermissionSet captions should remain within a defined maximum length to avoid issues in translated captions. When no MaxLength or Locked subproperty is specified, translations can exceed the intended limit and cause inconsistent or truncated UI labels.

data.name: PermissionSetCaptionLengthCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Set MaxLength property to 30

data.name: PermissionSetCoverageTitle
data.value: All application objects must be covered by a PermissionSet

data.name: PermissionSetCoverageMessageFormat
data.value: The application object {0} '{1}' is not covered by any PermissionSet in this extension.

data.name: PermissionSetCoverageDescription
data.value: Every application object in an extension must be explicitly granted permissions through at least one permission set or permission set extension, defined in AL or XML.

data.name: PublicEventPublisherTitle
data.value: Event publisher methods should not be public.

data.name: PublicEventPublisherMessageFormat
data.value: Event publisher method '{0}' should be declared as local or internal. Public event publishers can be called by external dependency apps and prevent future parameter extensions without introducing breaking changes.

data.name: PublicEventPublisherDescription
data.value: Event publisher methods are intended solely to raise events within the extension that defines them. When declared as public, they can be called by external dependency apps, which is a design smell and breaks the intended event-driven pattern. Additionally, public event publishers are treated as part of the public API, meaning their parameter signature cannot be extended in the future without introducing breaking changes.

data.name: PublicEventPublisherCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Make event publisher method local

data.name: RunPageImplementPageManagementTitle
data.value: Use the "Page Management" codeunit instead invoking Page.Run directly

data.name: RunPageImplementPageManagementMessageFormat
data.value: Invoke pages through the "Page Management" codeunit instead of invoking Page.Run(...) directly for this record context.

data.name: RunPageImplementPageManagementDescription
data.value: Direct calls to Page.Run bypass the centralized logic provided by the Page Management codeunit. Using Page Management improves correctness and extensibility by automatically resolving the appropriate page.

data.name: RunPageImplementPageManagementCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Refactor to use "Page Management" codeunit

data.name: TableDataAccessRequiresPermissionsTitle
data.value: Table data access requires explicit object permissions

data.name: TableDataAccessRequiresPermissionsMessageFormat
data.value: The object does not declare permission "{0}" for tabledata "{1}"

data.name: TableDataAccessRequiresPermissionsDescription
data.value: Code that reads from or writes to table data must declare the required Permissions on the containing object. This ensures the code can run using indirect permissions, which is required when table access is restricted by the user's license.

data.name: TableDataAccessRequiresPermissionsCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Add missing permissions

data.name: TableDataPerCompanyDeclarationTitle
data.value: DataPerCompany must be explicitly set on table objects

data.name: TableDataPerCompanyDeclarationMessageFormat
data.value: The table object does not explicitly define the DataPerCompany property.

data.name: TableDataPerCompanyDeclarationDescription
data.value: In projects where company data isolation matters, table objects must explicitly define the DataPerCompany property. Relying on implicit defaults makes the data scope unclear and can lead to incorrect assumptions about whether data is shared across companies or stored per company.

data.name: TableFieldToolTipShouldBeDefinedTitle
data.value: Table field must define a ToolTip.

data.name: TableFieldToolTipShouldBeDefinedMessageFormat
data.value: A value for the ToolTip property is missing for the table field '{0}' of page field '{1}'. Consider adding a ToolTip for table field '{0}' and/or removing the ToolTip from page field '{1}'.

data.name: TableFieldToolTipShouldBeDefinedDescription
data.value: Informs the user that it is better to apply the ToolTip property on the table field instead of the page field.

data.name: ToolTipDoNotUseLineBreaksTitle
data.value: Do not use line breaks in ToolTip

data.name: ToolTipDoNotUseLineBreaksMessageFormat
data.value: ToolTip text must not contain line breaks.

data.name: ToolTipDoNotUseLineBreaksDescription
data.value: The Business Central user assistance model does not support formatting in ToolTips. Line breaks are not rendered and make the ToolTip harder to read. Keep ToolTips as a single, continuous sentence.

data.name: ToolTipMaximumLengthTitle
data.value: ToolTip should not exceed 200 characters

data.name: ToolTipMaximumLengthMessageFormat
data.value: ToolTip exceeds the recommended maximum length of 200 characters.

data.name: ToolTipMaximumLengthDescription
data.value: The user assistance model recommends keeping ToolTips short (about 200 characters including spaces) so users can scan them quickly. The UI can render longer ToolTips, but longer text reduces scannability and consistency.

data.name: ToolTipMustEndWithDotTitle
data.value: ToolTip must end with a dot

data.name: ToolTipMustEndWithDotMessageFormat
data.value: ToolTip must end with a dot.

data.name: ToolTipMustEndWithDotDescription
data.value: End ToolTips with a dot to keep phrasing consistent and clearly separated from surrounding UI text.

data.name: ToolTipShouldStartWithSpecifiesTitle
data.value: ToolTip should start with 'Specifies'

data.name: ToolTipShouldStartWithSpecifiesMessageFormat
data.value: ToolTip should start with the verb 'Specifies'.

data.name: ToolTipShouldStartWithSpecifiesDescription
data.value: For field ToolTips, the Business Central user assistance model expects the text to start with the verb 'Specifies' to describe what the field represents.

data.name: UseReturnValueForDatabaseReadMethodsTitle
data.value: Use return value for better error handling

data.name: UseReturnValueForDatabaseReadMethodsMessageFormat
data.value: The return value of the '{0}' method must be used to improve error handling or provide meaningful feedback to the user.

data.name: UseReturnValueForDatabaseReadMethodsDescription
data.value: Database read methods, like Record.Get(), returns a boolean indicating whether the record was successfully retrieved. Failing to use this return value can lead to uncaught errors, poor error handling, and a lack of actionable feedback for users when something goes wrong.

data.name: ZeroEnumValueReservedForEmptyTitle
data.value: Reserve Enum value zero (0) for empty value

data.name: ZeroEnumValueReservedForEmptyMessageFormat
data.value: Enum '{0}' with value 0 must have an empty Name and Caption.

data.name: ZeroEnumValueReservedForEmptyDescription
data.value: Zero (0) should be reserved as the empty Enum value. Business Central stores Enums as integers and does not support null; new records (and existing records after adding a field via table extension) default to 0, which makes non-empty meaning at 0 ambiguous.
